## Perspective Audit Summary

**Component/artifact reviewed:** Navigation Menu With Complete Landmarks  
**Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Screen Reader Semantic  
**Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification & Reflow, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular & Motion, Auditory Access, Keyboard & Motor, Cognitive & Neurodivergent

### Findings by Severity

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| CRITICAL | 2 |

### Perspectives That Passed

- Magnification & Reflow (not reviewed)
- Environmental Contrast (not reviewed)
- Vestibular & Motion (not reviewed)
- Auditory Access (not reviewed)
- Keyboard & Motor (not reviewed)
- Cognitive & Neurodivergent (not reviewed)

### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role

**Front-End Dev**
- **Missing `<title>` element** — Screen Reader Semantic — CRITICAL — WCAG 2.4.2 Page Title
- **Missing `lang` attribute on `<html>`** — Screen Reader Semantic — CRITICAL — WCAG 3.1.1 Language of Page

### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification

None

### Recommended Next Step

BLOCK (CRITICAL findings present — do not ship until resolved)

---

## Detailed Findings

```
**Finding:** Page lacks `<title>` element to identify content
**WCAG:** 2.4.2 Page Title
**Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design)
**Evidence:** No `<title>` found in document head
**Fix:** Add `<title>Widget Pro | Acme Corp</title>` to document head

**Finding:** Missing `lang` attribute on `<html>` element
**WCAG:** 3.1.1 Language of Page
**Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic
**Severity:** CRITICAL
**Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author)
**Evidence:** `<html>` tag has no `lang` attribute
**Fix:** Add `lang="en"` to `<html>` tag
```

---

---

## Passed Checklist Items

- [PASS] Landmark regions present and correctly used: `<main>`, `<nav>`, `<header>`, `<footer>`, `<aside>`
- [PASS] Heading hierarchy is logical with `<h1>` present and descriptive
- [PASS] All informative images have appropriate `alt` (logo has `aria-label`)
- [PASS] DOM/source order matches visual reading order
- [PASS] Link text describes destination (no "click here")
- [PASS] ARIA roles used correctly with distinct `aria-label` for navigation
- [PASS] Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements
- [PASS] Breadcrumb trail uses `aria-current="page"` on current item
- [PASS] All links have visible focus styles with ≥3:1 contrast

---

## Red Flags Checked

- No missing `aria-label` on custom widgets (not applicable)
- No missing `aria-describedby` on form fields (not applicable)
- No missing `aria-live` regions (not applicable)
- No missing `aria-labelledby` (used correctly on sections)

---

## Evidence of Compliance

- Landmark audit: 4 `<nav>` elements with distinct `aria-label`, `<main>` with `id="main-content"`, `<aside>` with `aria-label`
- Heading outline: `<h1>` for page title, `<h2>` for sections with `aria-labelledby`
- Image audit: Logo has `aria-label="Acme Corp home"`
- Focus audit: All links have visible focus styles with 3px outlines
- Navigation audit: 4 distinct `<nav>` landmarks with clear roles

---

## Required Fixes Before Shipping

1. Add `<title>` element to document head
2. Add `lang="en"` to `<html>` tag

These fixes are critical for screen reader users to:
- Identify the page's purpose through the title
- Have content read in the correct language
- Navigate the document structure effectively
